text.text-2: Building Family Wealth and Cultural Continuity on Country
text.text-3: Traditional Owners of Loves Creek Station and Oonchiumpa Consultancy and Services
text.text-4: Supported by A Curious Tractor. A ten-year pathway for intergenerational opportunity, cultural leadership, and community-led enterprise in and around Alice Springs.
text.text-6: We acknowledge the Traditional Owners of Atnarpa and Loves Creek Station, and we pay our respects to Elders past and present. This proposal is our commitment to care for Country and strengthen culture, community, and family through our own leadership.
text.text-9: Our family legacy carries responsibility and pride. The homestead and surrounding Country hold both the weight of history and the strength of survival.
text.text-10: "We know what we wanna do on our land, and we know how to get there." - Kristy Bloomfield
text.text-11: We continue this work through cultural authority, practical delivery, and shared commitment to future generations.
text.text-12: Family history and continuity at Atnarpa
text.text-14: From Pastoralist to Tourism and On-Country Learning
text.text-15: Our ancestors shared knowledge through pastoral life, station work, and family teaching. That practice continues through cultural tourism, educational immersion, and intergenerational transfer of knowledge.
text.text-16: Knowledge sharing from station life to visitor engagement
text.text-17: On-country educational training and youth learning
text.text-19: Land claim recognition and community ceremony
text.text-22: Land rights recognition enables us to lead our own future at Atnarpa. It allows restoration, activation, and long-term planning under Traditional Owner leadership.
text.text-23: Today, we continue sharing culture with care and invite respectful participation guided by our rules and values.
text.text-24: "Our kids 10 years time, that's our focus at the moment." - Kylie Bloomfield
text.text-27: The previous build week and partnership context is documented in this story from A Curious Tractor. It can sit beside this pitch as supporting evidence of delivery and trust.
text.text-31: These clips show day-to-day work, land care, and family-led activity on station. Videos are click-to-play.
text.text-34: "This week has made a huge impact on our family and creating our own history."
text.text-36: "Growing up here, mate, it's God's country."
text.text-38: "We know what we wanna do on our land, and we know how to get there."
text.text-40: "Our kids 10 years time, that's our focus at the moment."
text.text-42: "It's just good to be coming home, to grow my kids up here."
text.text-44: "What they created here with us this week was unbelievable."
text.text-49: Complete design, approvals, and site-enabling works while launching early cultural and youth programs.
text.text-51: Deliver the function space, covered connections, and essential services. Open for education, events, and tourism activity.
text.text-53: Scale programs, refine operations, and transfer leadership pathways across family and community.
text.text-57: Cultural learning, storytelling, and identity-strengthening led by Traditional Owners and mentors.
text.text-59: Skill development for employment in hospitality, land care, logistics, and cultural program delivery.
text.text-61: Shared gatherings, intergenerational exchange, and respectful visitor engagement on Country.
text.text-63: Story sharing and locally led offerings that build family income and protect cultural authority.
text.text-64: Function room and gathering space context
text.text-65: Activated event and workshop area concept
text.text-68: Use these fields to enter base build costings and dream-plan extensions for each delivery area. Figures can be revised live with partners and contractors.
text.text-70: Core enclosed facility for workshops, events, and year-round operation.
text.text-72: Shaded transition zone with airflow, fan points, and protected circulation.
text.text-73: Ground Preparation, Boulders, Access and Services
text.text-74: Site leveling, drainage, heavy access, cultural landscape treatment, and utility readiness.
text.text-76: Outdoor event spaces for dining, storytelling, training, and visitor hosting.
text.text-78: On-country programming, youth pathways, delivery staffing, and annual operations.
text.text-80: Build Zone Overlay and Flyover Planner
text.text-81: Use this drone image to map build zones, trace boundaries, and connect each zone to flyover footage. You can draw directly over the image while planning.
text.text-82: Pin Guide: A Function Hall Precinct, B Services Cluster, C Covered Link Spine, D Arrival and Cultural Welcome, E Gathering and Event Edge.
text.text-88: Workshop attendance, program completion, and event engagement across age groups.
text.text-90: Youth training uptake and progression into employment and leadership roles.
text.text-92: Site-readiness milestones, build completion stages, and ongoing operational stability.
text.text-95: Atnarpa is a place of memory and possibility. We are seeking partnership that strengthens our capacity to deliver our own vision over the next ten years.
text.text-96: We invite Snow Foundation to walk with us as we build family wealth, cultural continuity, and opportunity for young people and the wider Alice Springs community.
text.text-97: "We know what we want to do on our land, and we know how to get there."
text.text-98: Visual draft prepared for Atnarpa proposal development. Designed for web review and PDF export.

zoneA.title: Zone A: Function Hall Precinct
zoneA.description: Primary location for enclosed function room, kitchen, and toilets. Use this as the anchor footprint for the first major build package.

photos.caption: Main function space footprint and mountain outlook.
Current edge condition for hall and event orientation.
Wider site reference for arrival and circulation planning.

zoneB.title: Zone B: Services Cluster
zoneB.description: Potential area for utility support, storage, and delivery access that backs the function room operations.

photos.caption: Existing services and temporary infrastructure layout.
Service-side condition for staging and access improvements.
Reference edge where utilities and event operations meet.

zoneC.title: Zone C: Covered Link Spine
zoneC.description: Shaded connection between structures, pedestrian flow, and all-weather movement corridor.

photos.caption: Potential line for covered connection and pathways.
Deck and transition precedent for shaded movement.
Ground condition reference for linking structures.

zoneD.title: Zone D: Arrival and Cultural Welcome
zoneD.description: Entry zone for orientation, welcome moments, and wayfinding into the active project area.

photos.caption: Arrival-facing context for welcome and storytelling points.
Family-led welcome and on-country hosting context.
Historical continuity reference for cultural entry narrative.

zoneE.title: Zone E: Gathering and Event Edge
zoneE.description: Outdoor seating, event spill-out, and landscape-based gathering spaces aligned to views and cultural use.

photos.caption: Existing event edge and deck precedent.
Gathering zone orientation with sunset and terrain.
Dinner and tourism activation precedent for event edge.
